method: RepoPrompt static analysis + grep/wc inspection (no LLM call)

findings.title: Duplicate router registration — two handlers for GET /api/v1/additives
findings.evidence: app/api/v1/resources.py:99 defines list_additives AND app/api/v1/additives.py:29 defines list_additives. Both are included in app/main.py:217-220. First registered wins (resources.py), meaning the richer version in additives.py (with category filter) is shadowed or ordering-dependent.
findings.impact: Filter by category silently drops; API contract non-deterministic; readers cannot tell which handler owns the route.
findings.fix: Delete list_additives from resources.py. Keep only additives.py. Update main.py to stop pretending resources.py owns additives (tag/router split is already confused).

findings.title: Stray directory in migrations/versions
findings.evidence: migrations/versions/'$(ls -t migrations'/ exists — a literal shell-expansion artifact committed to the repo.
findings.impact: Confuses Alembic revision discovery on some filesystems; signals a botched command was committed.
findings.fix: rm -rf "migrations/versions/\$(ls -t migrations" and verify alembic history is clean.

findings.title: Two dead placeholder functions in app/services/validation.py
findings.evidence: validate_oil_ids_exist() at L221 and validate_additive_ids() at L237 both end with `pass` and have 'Placeholder - will be implemented in endpoint' comments. The real logic was inlined into calculate.py instead.
findings.impact: Dead code with a misleading name/signature; any future caller will get None back and silently pass. Violates truth-first standard.
findings.fix: Delete both functions. If reuse is desired, extract the real implementation from calculate.py into the service.

findings.title: Two ways to import get_db — inconsistent
findings.evidence: Six routers use `from app.db.base import get_db`; inci.py uses `from app.db import get_db`. app/db/__init__.py re-exports it, so both work but the project disagrees with itself.
findings.impact: Low functional impact, but shows a style inconsistency that will grow. Pick one and enforce via ruff.
findings.fix: Standardize on `from app.db import get_db` (the package-level API) and update six files. Add an isort/ruff rule.

findings.title: List-endpoint boilerplate duplicated across 4 routers (~100 lines of copy-paste)
findings.evidence: resources.py (oils+additives), additives.py, colorants.py, essential_oils.py each repeat the same pattern: build select → apply search ilike → count via subquery → apply sort via getattr → paginate → return {items, total_count, limit, offset, has_more}.
findings.impact: Any bug (e.g., search behavior, count semantics) must be fixed in 5 places. Recent evidence: CQ-01 duplication stems from this.
findings.fix: Extract a generic `async def paginated_list(db, model, *, search_fields, filters, sort_by, sort_order, limit, offset, item_schema)` helper in app/api/_pagination.py. Each router becomes ~20 lines.

findings.title: Pydantic v1 from_orm used with Pydantic 2.5+
findings.evidence: resources.py, additives.py, colorants.py, essential_oils.py call `Item.from_orm(obj)`. Pydantic v2 deprecated from_orm in favor of model_validate with from_attributes=True.
findings.impact: Deprecation warnings in tests/logs; may break on a future Pydantic minor bump.
findings.fix: Replace with `ItemSchema.model_validate(obj)` after setting `model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)` on each list-item schema. Do this while extracting the pagination helper (CQ-05).

findings.title: Broad `except Exception` swallows real errors
findings.evidence: app/api/v1/calculate.py:548 catches bare Exception around the entire POST /calculate body. app/core/security.py:58 verify_password catches Exception and returns False (masks argon2 errors). app/api/v1/calculate.py:530-547 health check catches Exception and returns raw str(e) to clients.
findings.impact: Hides bugs, complicates debugging, leaks internal error text in health response. The pattern `except Exception: return False` in auth is a silent-failure anti-pattern.
findings.fix: Narrow exceptions — HashingError for passlib, SQLAlchemyError for DB. Log the exception with context before re-raising or returning a structured 500. Never str(e) into client JSON for a health check.

findings.title: calculate.py is 556 lines — single 480-line request handler
findings.evidence: app/api/v1/calculate.py:66 create_calculation spans ~380 lines, doing oil validation, DB fetches, lye/water/metrics/fatty-acid calculations, additive effect assembly, persistence, and response shaping in one function.
findings.impact: Hard to unit-test (requires full DB + HTTP context); hard to reason about failure modes; blocks CQ-07 fix.
findings.fix: Split into: (1) `_resolve_inputs(request, db)` → (oils_dict, additives_dict, normalized), (2) `_compute_recipe(normalized, oils_dict, additives_dict)` → pure dataclass, (3) `_persist_calculation(computed, user, db)`. Handler becomes orchestration only.

findings.title: two_format_inci_generator leaks Decimal(str(float)) pattern 8 times
findings.evidence: app/services/three_format_inci_generator.py lines 53,57,62,63,116,137,158,169 all do `Decimal(str(value))` on dict lookups. Indicates the caller is passing floats but the function wants Decimal.
findings.impact: Every converter is a potential source of rounding drift; the contract is fuzzy — is the input Decimal, float, or str?
findings.fix: Type the `recipe_data` dict as a TypedDict with Decimal fields and have callers pass Decimals. Remove the defensive Decimal(str(...)) wrappers at the boundary, do it once at the seam.

findings.title: Dockerfile.debian-backup committed
findings.evidence: Dockerfile.debian-backup at repo root, 0 references in code/playbooks/docs.
findings.impact: Cargo-cult leftover from the UBI migration; confuses readers about which Dockerfile is authoritative.
findings.fix: Delete. Git preserves it in history if needed.

findings.title: Coverage-on-every-test is wasteful in dev loop
findings.evidence: pytest.ini addopts = -v --cov=app --cov-report=term-missing --cov-report=html. pyproject.toml repeats the same. Every `pytest tests/unit/test_one_thing.py` regenerates an HTML coverage report.
findings.impact: Slow feedback loop; TDD cycle time suffers.
findings.fix: Move coverage to a `[tool.coverage]` section and a `pytest --cov` invocation in CI only. Default `pytest` should be just `-v`. Or add a `make cov` / nox target.

findings.title: Schema module split doesn't match router split
findings.evidence: app/schemas/requests.py + responses.py hold calculation contracts. But additive.py, colorant.py, essential_oil.py, resource.py, inci_label.py are per-domain. Two organizing principles coexist.
findings.impact: Pydantic models drift; e.g., AdditiveListItem exists in both schemas/additive.py and schemas/resource.py and they're probably different.
findings.fix: Commit to one principle: per-domain modules. Fold requests.py/responses.py into calculation.py. Dedupe the two AdditiveListItem variants.

findings.title: Package name drift — pyproject says mga-automotive, product is Midnight Garden Apothecary
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml: authors = 'MGA Automotive', email = info@mga-automotive.com. main.py contact block says MGA Automotive. License: 'Proprietary - MGA Automotive'. Actual business (per CLAUDE.md) is Midnight Garden Apothecary under REC.
findings.impact: Misleads contributors; legal identity in the license line is wrong.
findings.fix: Update pyproject.toml, main.py contact/license, README.md authors block, Dockerfile LABEL to Midnight Garden Apothecary / correct email.

findings.title: No ruff/mypy gate in CI visible at repo root
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/soap-calculator-ci.yml exists but pyproject.toml declares strict mypy (disallow_untyped_defs) and ruff config. Needs verification they run pre-merge.
findings.impact: Strict config doesn't help if CI doesn't enforce.
findings.fix: Read the workflow; if ruff/mypy not present, add jobs. If they are, ignore this finding.
